terms.label: psychoanalysis
terms.definition: Psychoanalysis is presented as a personality tradition centred on conflict, development, unconscious meaning, and layered organisation of the person.
terms.course_role: Marks the first major theoretical bloc that departs sharply from trait and assessment models.

representative_excerpts: Psykoanalysens personlighedsbegreb (Forelæsning 7, 2026-02-23)
Kilderne samler klassiske psykoanalytiske formuleringer om drift, konflikt og personlighedsstruktur.
Gammelgaard diskuterer psykoanalytiske forståelser af personlighed med vægt på subjektivitet og relationel dynamik.
Psykoanalytisk personlighedsstruktur (Forelæsning 8, 2026-02-24)
He argues that tensions in concepts like drive, ego, and object should be interpreted dialectically as clues to the object of psychoanalysis.
8. Psykoanalytisk personlighedsstruktur
Empiri og metode i psykoanalysen (Forelæsning 9, 2026-03-02)
Forelæsningen samler klassiske og nyere diskussioner af, hvad der kan tælle som empiri i psykoanalysen.
Psykoanalysens metode fremstilles derfor som en fortolkende praksis, der må begrunde sine sandhedskrav uden at reducere subjektet til målbare størrelser.
Psykoanalysens konstituering (Forelæsning 10, 2026-03-03)
Forelæsningen samler nyere psykoanalytiske teorier om, hvordan subjektet konstitueres gennem drifter, objektrelationer og det ubevidste.
På tværs af teksterne fremstilles psykoanalysen som et felt, hvor konstitutionen af personlighed må tænkes relationelt, strukturelt og historisk.

terms.label: stability and change
terms.definition: Stability and change are treated as joint problems: personality must explain durable patterning without denying development, revision, and historical variation.
terms.course_role: Functions as one of the core comparison questions across the whole semester.
lecture_titles: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)
Sammenligning af teorier og subjektets historie og eksamen (Forelæsning 22, 2026-04-27)

representative_excerpts: Grundbogsteksten samler de centrale faglige stridspunkter om stabilitet, forandring og forklaringsniveau.
Kontinuitet og forandring bør forstås som samtidige processer.
Personlighed fremstilles både som mønstret kontinuitet og kontekstfølsom forandring.

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)
representative_excerpts: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)
This meta-analysis synthesizes longitudinal evidence on personality stability and mean-level change across the lifespan.
It confirms substantial rank-order stability while documenting systematic maturation-linked change patterns.
Forandring kan beskrives som narrativ revision over tid.
Forandring kan forstås som omorganisering af livsfortællingen.
Identity stability is achieved through ongoing coordination work.

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Sammenligning af teorier og subjektets historie og eksamen (Forelæsning 22, 2026-04-27)
representative_excerpts: Analyse bør forbinde biografi med historisk forandring.
The text situates psychological development within macro-sociological change.

terms.label: critical psychology
terms.definition: Critical psychology situates subjectivity, action, and difficulty within historical practices and structural conditions rather than inside isolated individuals.
terms.course_role: Expands the critique of individualisation into a broader sociohistorical theory of subjectivity and participation.

representative_excerpts: Critical psychology (Forelæsning 16, 2026-03-24)
Forelæsningen samler kritisk psykologi med fokus på subjektivitet, samfundsforhold og handlemuligheder.
Holzkamp udvikler et grundlag for kritisk psykologi ved at sætte subjektivitet, samfundsmæssige vilkår og handlemuligheder i centrum.
Kritisk psykologi (Forelæsning 17, 2026-04-07)
Forelæsningen kobler praksisteori med kritisk psykologi i en relationel forståelse af subjektivitet og forandring.
Grundbog kapitel 10 - Kritisk psykologi

terms.label: personality traits
terms.definition: Traits are treated as relatively stable patterns of individual difference that can organise comparison across persons without exhausting the person.
terms.course_role: Serves as a central reference point that later traditions refine, criticise, or contrast.
lecture_titles: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)
Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)
representative_excerpts: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)
This text examines distinctions and relations between personality traits, states, and broader dimensions.
Traits and states should be modeled as connected, not opposed.

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)
representative_excerpts: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)
Mean-level change tends toward greater maturity on several traits.
Li and Wilt analyze person-situation interplay in the expression and development of individual differences.
McAdams and Pals propose an integrative model that combines dispositional traits, characteristic adaptations, and life narratives.
Traits, adaptations, and narratives represent distinct but connected levels.

terms.label: meaning-making
terms.definition: Meaning-making is treated as a practical, relational, and interpretive process through which personality becomes coherent across experience and time.
terms.course_role: Links phenomenological, humanistic, and narrative traditions around lived interpretation.

representative_excerpts: Analysen flyttes fra abstrakte variabler til personens konkrete relation til verden, krop og mening.
Mening opstår i personens praktiske engagement i verden.
Kapitlet udvikler en fænomenologisk tilgang til personlighed med udgangspunkt i levet erfaring og mening.
Personcentrerede perspektiver prioriterer erfaring, mening og valg.
He claims the field still relies too heavily on natural-science assumptions and needs methods that fit lived human meaning.
Mainstream psychological methods can miss specifically human meanings and contexts.
Narrativitet, kultur og mening (Forelæsning 21, 2026-04-21)
Kilderne introducerer narrativ psykologi, hvor personlighed forstås gennem fortælling, tid og kulturel meningsdannelse.
Bruner argues that human meaning is fundamentally narrative and culturally organized.

terms.label: existential personality psychology
terms.definition: Existential personality psychology frames personality through freedom, finitude, relation, anxiety, and the person's stance toward existence.
terms.course_role: Deepens the experiential turn by foregrounding existential conditions rather than trait profiles.

representative_excerpts: Eksistentiel personlighedspsykologi I (Funch) (Forelæsning 12, 2026-03-10)
Forelæsningen samler eksistentielle perspektiver på personlighed, angst og frihed.
Lidelse kan beskrives som begrænsede eksistentielle muligheder.
Eksistentiel personlighedspsykologi II (Bjerregaard-Nielsen) (Forelæsning 13, 2026-03-16)
Kilderne samler eksistentielle og fænomenologiske bidrag til moderne personlighedsforståelse.
Eksistentielle temaer organiserer mange kliniske problemstillinger.

terms.label: narrativity
terms.definition: Narrativity treats personality and identity as organised through stories, temporal ordering, and culturally shared meaning structures.
terms.course_role: Offers a late-course alternative to trait and mechanism models by making interpretation and life history central.
lecture_titles: Sammenligning af teorier og subjektets historie og eksamen (Forelæsning 22, 2026-04-27)

representative_excerpts: Narrativitet, kultur og mening (Forelæsning 21, 2026-04-21)
Forelæsningen kontrasterer narrative perspektiver med træk- og mekanismeorienterede modeller.
Narrativer organiserer hukommelse, motivforståelse og oplevet kontinuitet.

terms.label: culture and personality
terms.definition: Culture enters the course as a constitutive context for personality development, norms, and the interpretation of selfhood.
terms.course_role: Links trait-oriented work to broader historical, relational, and narrative understandings of persons.

representative_excerpts: Culture, evolution, genetics, and personality (Forelæsning 5, 2026-02-16)
Forelæsningen forbinder kultur, evolution og genetik i en flerfaktoriel forståelse af personlighed.
Der argumenteres for multimodale designs, som bedre kan fange gen-miljø-samspil og kulturel variation.
Narrativitet, kultur og mening (Forelæsning 21, 2026-04-21)
Kilderne introducerer narrativ psykologi, hvor personlighed forstås gennem fortælling, tid og kulturel meningsdannelse.
Livsfortællinger samskabes i kulturelle og relationelle sammenhænge.

terms.label: person-situation interplay
terms.definition: The course uses person-situation interplay to show that personality must be understood in the movement between patterned dispositions and concrete contexts.
terms.course_role: Acts as a bridge from early differential models toward more relational and practice-based approaches.
lecture_titles: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)
representative_excerpts: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)
Person-situation-perspektivet viser, at adfærd opstår i koblingen mellem disposition og kontekst.
Udvikling forstås bedst som et samspil mellem person og situation.
He argues that psychological phenomena are distributed across contexts rather than localized inside individuals.
Persons move through multiple practice contexts with shifting positions.
Analysis must follow cross-context movement over time.

terms.label: humanistic psychology
terms.definition: Humanistic psychology treats persons as oriented toward growth, authenticity, relation, and context-sensitive development rather than deficit alone.
terms.course_role: Provides a positive, experience-near alternative to reductionist or control-oriented models.

representative_excerpts: Humanistisk psykologi (Forelæsning 14, 2026-03-17)
Forelæsningen behandler humanistisk psykologi som et alternativ til reduktionistiske personmodeller.
Humanistisk psykologi udfordrer underskuds- og kontrolmodeller.

terms.label: phenomenology
terms.definition: Phenomenology reorients personality toward lived experience, embodiment, and the person's concrete relation to world and meaning.
terms.course_role: Begins the semester's move toward first-person, experiential, and interpretive approaches.

representative_excerpts: Fænomenologi (Forelæsning 11, 2026-03-09)
Kilderne udvikler en fænomenologisk tilgang til personlighed med udgangspunkt i levet erfaring.
Grundbog kapitel 04 - Fænomenologisk personlighedspsykologi

terms.label: personality assessment
terms.definition: The course treats personality assessment as the problem of how personality is operationalised, measured, and interpreted across methods and contexts.
terms.course_role: Frames the early methodological discussion about what counts as evidence for personality.

representative_excerpts: Personality assessment (Forelæsning 2, 2026-02-03)
Samlet peger teksterne på, at vurderingskvalitet kræver tydelig kobling mellem måling, teori og inferens.
Personlighedsmåling er teori-afhængig fra start til slut.

terms.label: personality dimensions
terms.definition: Dimensions express personality as patterned continua rather than isolated categories, while still raising questions about interpretation at person level.
terms.course_role: Connects psychometric modelling to later debates about functioning, structure, and categorisation.
lecture_titles: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)
representative_excerpts: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)
It discusses how latent dimensions are inferred and what kinds of behavioral regularities they capture.
Measurement decisions influence apparent dimensionality.
Sharp and Wall examine personality functioning and disturbance through dimensional, developmental lenses.
Personality pathology is better treated dimensionally than categorically.

terms.label: poststructuralism
terms.definition: Poststructuralism treats subjects as historically produced through discourse, power, ethics, and positioning rather than as pre-given inner essences.
terms.course_role: Provides the course's strongest language-and-power account of subject formation.
lecture_titles: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)

representative_excerpts: Sociokulturelle teorier - socialkonstruktionisme og poststrukturalisme (Forelæsning 19, 2026-04-14)
Foucault omformulerer etik som praksisser for selvforhold i historiske magtrelationer.
Foucault (1997)

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)
representative_excerpts: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)

terms.label: køn
terms.definition: Gender is approached as a psychosocial and historical process rather than a fixed person-variable, linking identity, norm, and institution.
terms.course_role: Brings embodiment, normativity, and social organisation into the course's personality debates.
lecture_titles: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)

representative_excerpts: Køn (Forelæsning 18, 2026-04-13)
Forelæsningen behandler køn som en psykosocial proces frem for et fast persontræk.
Kilderne viser, hvordan femininitet og maskulinitet formes i samspillet mellem intime erfaringer og institutionelle rammer.

terms.label: personality structure
terms.definition: Structure names the organisation of psychic life that shapes how conflict, relation, and regulation are sustained over time.
terms.course_role: Connects psychoanalytic organisation to broader questions of functioning and pathology.

representative_excerpts: Psykoanalytisk personlighedsstruktur (Forelæsning 8, 2026-02-24)
Forelæsningen flytter fokus fra ego-centrerede modeller til et strukturelt syn på subjektivitet.
Lacan analyzes subjectivity through language, symbolic structure, and desire.

terms.label: critical personalism
terms.definition: Critical personalism resists reducing persons to abstract variable profiles and insists on person-level interpretation, action, and conceptual caution.
terms.course_role: Acts as a direct internal critique of reified trait language without abandoning systematic psychology.

representative_excerpts: Critical personalism (Forelæsning 15, 2026-03-23)
Kilderne introducerer kritisk personalisme som opgør med reificerede trækbegreber.
Critical personalism re-centers agency and context in personality theory.

terms.label: subjectivation
terms.definition: Subjectivation names the process by which persons become particular kinds of subjects through discourse, power, relation, and practice.
terms.course_role: Connects poststructural, feminist, and critical approaches around a shared process view of subject formation.
lecture_titles: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)

representative_excerpts: Kilderne samler poststrukturalistiske perspektiver på etik, subjektivering og magt.
Davies viser, hvordan subjektet bliver til gennem diskurser og positionering i sociale praksisser.
The interview links truth, power, and subjectivation to everyday ethical work on conduct.

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)
representative_excerpts: Personlighed beskrives som løbende subjektivering, historisk organiseret gennem sociale og politiske ordener.
Grundbog kapitel 11 - Postpsykologisk subjektiveringsteori
Kapitlet omformulerer personlighed som løbende subjektivering frem for en fast indre kerne.

terms.label: first-person perspective
terms.definition: The first-person perspective is treated as a legitimate source of psychological knowledge rather than a residual supplement to external measurement.
terms.course_role: Supplies a methodological pivot away from objectifying models of the person.

representative_excerpts: Kilderne udvikler en fænomenologisk tilgang til personlighed med udgangspunkt i levet erfaring.
Førstepersonsperspektivet er en legitim og nødvendig videnskilde.
Kapitlet udvikler en fænomenologisk tilgang til personlighed med udgangspunkt i levet erfaring og mening.
Det humanistiske perspektiv placerer levet erfaring og agenthed centralt i personlighedsteori.
Person-centered perspectives prioritize lived experience and agency.

terms.label: personality functioning
terms.definition: Personality functioning is used to shift attention from simple diagnostic labels toward the organisation, coherence, and impairment of personality processes.
terms.course_role: Introduces the pathology block and broadens dimensional thinking toward clinical structure.

representative_excerpts: Personlighedsfunktion og forstyrrelse (Forelæsning 6, 2026-02-17)
Sharp and Wall examine personality functioning and disturbance through dimensional, developmental lenses.
They discuss how impairments in self and interpersonal functioning relate to personality pathology.

terms.label: social constructionism
terms.definition: Social constructionism shifts the analysis of personality toward shared practices, language, and historically situated forms of reality-making.
terms.course_role: Marks the move from person-centred explanation toward socially constituted subjectivity.
lecture_titles: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)

representative_excerpts: Sociokulturelle teorier - socialkonstruktionisme og poststrukturalisme (Forelæsning 19, 2026-04-14)

terms.label: feminist theory
terms.definition: Feminist theory brings gendered power, embodiment, and historically unequal positioning into the interpretation of personality and subjectivity.
terms.course_role: Extends the course's social critique by showing how personhood is organised through gendered relations.
lecture_titles: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)

representative_excerpts: Feminist critique and psychoanalytic theory can be combined rather than opposed.

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)
representative_excerpts: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)

terms.label: evolutionary perspective
terms.definition: The evolutionary perspective frames some personality variation as adaptive patterning shaped by long-run selection pressures.
terms.course_role: Supplies one of the course's strongest non-hermeneutic explanatory models.

representative_excerpts: Culture, evolution, genetics, and personality (Forelæsning 5, 2026-02-16)
Forelæsningen forbinder kultur, evolution og genetik i en flerfaktoriel forståelse af personlighed.
Volk and Puchalski discuss evolutionary perspectives on personality variation and adaptive trade-offs.

terms.label: genetic perspective
terms.definition: Genetic perspectives in the course treat part of personality variation as biologically constrained while still raising interpretation problems around mechanism and context.
terms.course_role: Keeps the biosocial block tied to questions of evidence, causality, and limitation.

representative_excerpts: Culture, evolution, genetics, and personality (Forelæsning 5, 2026-02-16)
This review synthesizes evidence that personality differences are shaped by many small genetic and environmental influences rather than single dominant causes.
Operationalization choices influence observed genetic and environmental effects.

terms.label: personality states
terms.definition: States mark the situational and time-sensitive side of personality that complicates purely trait-based explanations.
terms.course_role: Keeps the early-course trait discussion connected to variability, context, and process.
lecture_titles: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)
representative_excerpts: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)
This text examines distinctions and relations between personality traits, states, and broader dimensions.
Traits and states should be modeled as connected, not opposed.

terms.label: discursive psychology
terms.definition: Discursive psychology treats psychological realities as enacted in language, interaction, and shared discursive practice.
terms.course_role: Operationalises the sociocultural turn at the level of method and analysis.
lecture_titles: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)
representative_excerpts: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)
